claim: For all integers n > 1, sigma(n) is at least n + 1, with equality exactly when n is prime.
For all integers n > 1, if n is composite then sigma(n) > n + 1.
For all primes p, sigma(p) = p + 1.
For all n > 1, sigma(n) - n is always at least 1.
For all composite n > 4, sigma(n) - n >= n.
For n = 6, sigma(6) - 6 = 6, i.e., sigma(6) = 12.
For all primes p, the quantity sigma(p) - p - 1 equals 0.
For composite n with smallest prime divisor p, sigma(n) >= n + p.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n)) or (n == 4) or all(n % i != 0 for i in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1)) or (sigma(n) >= n + min([i for i in range(2, n) if n % i == 0]))

claim: For all n = p^2 where p is an odd prime, sigma(n) = 1 + p + p^2.
predicate: not any(is_prime(p) and p > 2 and n == p*p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1)) or (sigma(n) == sum([p**i for i in range(3) for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) if is_prime(p) and n == p*p]))

claim: For all n with exactly two distinct prime divisors, sigma(n) > 2n.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n)) or (len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 2) or (sigma(n) > 2 * n)

claim: For all n > 1, sigma(n) >= 2n if n is composite.
For all n = 2k where k > 1 is odd, sigma(n) - n >= k.
For n = p^3 where p is an odd prime, sigma(n) exceeds 2n.
predicate: not any(is_prime(p) and p > 2 and n == p**3 for p in range(2, int(n**(1/3))+2)) or (sigma(n) > 2*n)

claim: For all odd primes p, sigma(p) is even.
For all odd primes p, p + 1 is even.
For n composite, the ratio sigma(n) / n is strictly greater than 1 plus the reciprocal of the smallest prime divisor.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n)) or (len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) == 0) or (sigma(n) > n * (1 + 1.0/min([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])))

claim: For n composite and n != 4, sigma(n)/n > 1 + 1/n.
For all perfect numbers n (where sigma(n) = 2n), n must be even.
The smallest perfect number is 6.
For all n > 1, the abundancy index sigma(n)/n is greater than 1.
For all n > 1, sigma(n) and n cannot be equal.
For all abundant numbers n (where sigma(n) > 2n), the abundancy exceeds 2.
For all n with exactly three prime factors (counted with multiplicity) and n composite, sigma(n) - n >= floor(n/3).
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n)) or (sum(vp(n, p) for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p)) != 3) or (sigma(n) - n >= n // 3)

claim: For n = 2p where p is an odd prime, sigma(n) = (1 + 2)(1 + p).
predicate: not any(is_prime(p) and p > 2 and n == 2*p for p in range(2, n)) or (sigma(n) == 3 * (1 + min([p for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p) and n == 2*p])))

claim: For n = 6 (i.e., 2·3), sigma(n) = 12.
For all n with at least two distinct prime divisors, sigma(n) - n >= n/2.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n)) or (len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) < 2) or (sigma(n) - n >= n // 2)

claim: For all odd numbers n, if n is prime then sigma(n) is even.
For all n that are powers of 2 greater than 2, sigma(n) = 2n - 1.
For n = 4, sigma(4) = 7.
For all n = 2^k with k >= 2, sigma(n) - n = n - 1.
predicate: not (n > 2 and n & (n - 1) == 0) or (sigma(n) - n == n - 1)

claim: For n = p1 * p2 * ... * pk with k distinct odd primes, sigma(n) / n > (1 + 1/p_min).
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n)) or (sigma(n) / n <= 1) or (sigma(n) > n * (1 + 1.0/min([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)])))

claim: For all n that are products of exactly two distinct primes p < q, we have sigma(n) >= (1+p)(1+q).
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n)) or (len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) != 2) or all(vp(n, p) <= 1 for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p)) or (sigma(n) >= all(range(2)))

claim: For all composite n, sigma(n) - n has at least one odd divisor.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n)) or any((sigma(n) - n) % (2*i + 1) == 0 for i in range(1, (sigma(n) - n)//2 + 1))

claim: For all odd prime powers p^k with k >= 2, sigma(p^k) exceeds 2p^k.
predicate: not any(is_prime(p) and p > 2 and k >= 2 and n == p**k for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1) for k in range(2, 10)) or (sigma(n) > 2*n)

claim: For all n = p^2 with p an odd prime, sigma(n) - n >= p^2 / 2.
predicate: not any(is_prime(p) and p > 2 and n == p*p for p in range(2, int(n**0.5)+1)) or (sigma(n) - n >= n / 2)

claim: For all n > 1, the sum of all proper divisors of n is strictly less than sigma(n).
predicate: (n <= 1) or (sum([d for d in range(1, n) if n % d == 0]) < sigma(n))

claim: For n with omega(n) > 1 (where omega counts distinct prime divisors), sigma(n)/n grows as log(log(n)).
predicate: (n <= 1) or (len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) <= 1) or (sigma(n) / n > 1.2)

claim: For all n > 1, if gcd(n, sigma(n)) = 1 then n is prime.
For all composite n, gcd(n, sigma(n)) > 1.
For all primes p, gcd(p, p+1) = 1.
For n = 2 * (odd prime), the deficiency sigma(n) - 2n is exactly -(n/2 - 1).
predicate: not any(is_prime(p) and p > 2 and n == 2*p for p in range(2, n)) or (sigma(n) - 2*n == -(n//2 - 1))

claim: For all n with bigomega(n) = 4 (exactly 4 prime factors with multiplicity), sigma(n) - n >= n/4.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n)) or (sum(vp(n, p) for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p)) != 4) or (sigma(n) - n >= n // 4)

claim: For all odd numbers n > 1, sigma(n) is strictly greater than n + 1.
For all odd composite n, sigma(n) - n >= n.
For all composite n >= 6 that are even, sigma(n) >= n + n/2.
For all composite n, sigma(n) and n share at least one common prime factor.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n)) or any(n % p == 0 and sigma(n) % p == 0 for p in range(2, n) if is_prime(p))

claim: For all odd composite n, sigma(n) - n is odd.
For all odd primes p, the value 2p + 1 cannot be prime (Sophie Germain failure).
For all highly composite n with at least 5 distinct prime factors, sigma(n) > 3n.
predicate: (n <= 1) or (is_prime(n)) or (len([p for p in range(2, n) if n % p == 0 and is_prime(p)]) < 5) or (sigma(n) > 3*n)

claim: For all n in the range [120, 130], sigma(n) exceeds 2n.
